name: 05_Game_Simulation_DQN_CartPole_v1_RL
query: Tengeneza mfumo wa kuaminika wa kufundisha wakala wa DQN kwa kutumia PyTorch katika mazingira ya OpenAI Gym kama CartPole-v1. Tekeleza DQN kwa kutumia PyTorch katika `src/model.py`. Mazingira yanapaswa kuanzishwa katika faili kuu, `src/main.py`. Shughulikia tofauti zozote za vipimo kati ya DQN na mazingira (kama ingekuwa inatokea ikiwa ID ya mazingira ya Gym imebadilishwa) kwa kurekodi hitilafu. Pia tafadhali rekodi kurudi baada ya kila kipindi hadi `results/metrics/` na uisasisha baada ya kila kipindi kipya kumalizika. Hifadhi kurudi kwa mwisho juu ya mchakato wa kipindi kama `results/figures/return_over_time.png` na mfano wa mwisho uliopata mafunzo kama `models/saved_models/dqn_model.pt`. Ikiwezekana, ongeza maelezo kwenye kurudi juu ya mchakato wa kipindi ili kuonyesha hatua muhimu.

requirements.criteria: Algoriti ya "DQN" imetekelezwa kwa kutumia PyTorch na imehifadhiwa katika `src/model.py`.
Mchoro wa kurudi kwa muda umechorwa, ukirekodi kurudi kwa kila kipindi, na umehifadhiwa kama `results/figures/reward_over_time.png`.
Kurudi kwa kila kipindi kunarekodiwa hadi `results/metrics/` na kusasishwa baada ya kila kipindi kumalizika.

preferences.criteria: Mfumo unapaswa kushughulikia tofauti za vipimo, ukirekodi masuala kwa urahisi wa kutatua hitilafu.
Mchoro wa kurudi juu ya mchakato wa kipindi una maelezo ya hatua muhimu.

judge_stats.criteria: Algoriti ya "DQN" imetekelezwa kwa kutumia PyTorch na imehifadhiwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not contain an actual DQN implementation. Instead, it contains only a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" — essentially a placeholder/stub with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder. There is no actual DQN neural network architecture, no replay buffer, no Q-value computation, no training logic, and no PyTorch `nn.Module` subclass. The file is explicitly labeled as a minimal placeholder rather than a real implementation. Although a `dqn_model.pt` file exists in `models/saved_models/`, there is no evidence it was generated by a legitimate DQN training process given the stub nature of `model.py`.

<UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/main.py` does not instantiate any OpenAI Gym environment. The file only contains a stub/placeholder implementation with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `main()` function that simply returns a dictionary. There is no `import gym` statement, no `gym.make(...)` call, and no actual CartPole-v1 environment setup anywhere in the code.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa kurudi kwa muda umechorwa, ukirekodi kurudi kwa kila kipindi, na umehifadhiwa kama `results/figures/reward_over_time.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/reward_over_time.png` exists in the project structure at the expected path (`results\figures\reward_over_time.png`), confirming that the reward-over-time plot was generated and saved correctly. The error shown in the file content is merely a reading/display error from the evaluation tool (related to image format detection during base64 encoding), not an indication that the file is missing or corrupt. The file's presence in the workspace tree confirms the requirement is met.

<SATISFIED>: The file `models/saved_models/dqn_model.pt` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree, and the file content note confirms the file exists (though no reader is available for `.pt` format). The requirement that the trained model be saved at this specific path has been met.

judge_stats.criteria: Kurudi kwa kila kipindi kunarekodiwa hadi `results/metrics/` na kusasishwa baada ya kila kipindi kumalizika.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `results/metrics/` directory exists in the project structure but contains no files. Neither `main.py` nor `model.py` contains any code that records episode rewards or writes data to `results/metrics/`. Both files are minimal placeholder implementations that only define keyword lists and stub functions, with no actual training loop, episode tracking, or file I/O operations. The trajectory evidence also confirms there is no evidence of any process writing episode returns to that directory.
